commands.repair_reason: - [P2] Human handling is needed for the final merge-message edit and maintainer acceptance of the mitigation scope; I found no narrow code repair for an automated worker.; Cleared: The diff only changes transcript repair logic and tests, with no dependency, workflow, package, credential, or external code-execution surface changes. (sha=ab20c91a613f0dcdb4a4b623ad077fc1fc55f125)
commands.review_summary:
The PR updates `repairToolUseResultPairing` to replace default synthetic missing-tool-result duplicates with real tool results and adds focused regression coverage for the duplicate ordering cases.

PR surface: Source +59, Tests +117. Total +176 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. the failure is source-reproducible: current main keeps the first duplicate span result without distinguishing synthetic from real, and the PR body includes before/after terminal output from a runner against the repair function. I did not execute the proof because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Human handling is needed for the final merge-message edit and maintainer acceptance of the mitigation scope; I found no narrow code repair for an automated worker.

target.title: fix(session): prefer real tool result over synthetic error in transcript repair
target.body:
## Summary

Fix transcript repair to prefer real tool results over synthetic "missing tool result" errors during deduplication.

When a channel (e.g. Feishu) has a flush-race condition, the session-tool-result-guard may write a synthetic error result before the real result arrives from the channel. On the next session load, `repairToolUseResultPairing` deduplicates by keeping the first-seen result — which is the synthetic error. The user sees "Something went wrong" even though the tool call actually succeeded.

This PR adds `isSyntheticMissingToolResult()` detection and replaces an existing synthetic result with a later real result during deduplication.

> **Note:** This is a **transcript-repair mitigation**. The underlying flush-race in the session-tool-result-guard is a separate root-cause concern tracked independently in #84134. This PR does **not** close that issue.

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `session-transcript-repair.ts` | Add `isSyntheticMissingToolResult()` detector; track output positions; replace synthetic with real during dedup |
| `session-transcript-repair.test.ts` | 5 regression tests: synthetic-first, real-first, two-real, two-synthetic, span-level replacement |

## Tests

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/session-transcript-repair.test.ts
 ✓ unit-fast ../../src/agents/session-transcript-repair.test.ts (40 tests) 5ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  40 passed (40)
```

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: When a synthetic "missing tool result" error is persisted before the real result arrives (Feishu channel flush race), the repair pass keeps the synthetic error and the user sees "Something went wrong" even though the tool call succeeded.

- **Real environment tested**: Local patched OpenClaw checkout at `ab20c91a613f`, macOS arm64, Node.js `v24.10.0`.

- **Failing proof before fix**: On upstream/main, `repairToolUseResultPairing` keeps the first-seen result in `spanResultsById`. When a synthetic error arrives before the real result (flush-race ordering), the synthetic error wins:

```
$ npx tsx proof-84419-runner.mts  # on upstream/main

--- Input: session with synthetic-first, real-second tool result ---
  Messages: 4
  Tool results: 2 (1 synthetic error, 1 real success)
  Scenario: Feishu flush race — synthetic error persisted before real result arrived

--- Output: after repair ---
  Messages: 4
  Tool results: 2
  Kept result isError: true
  Kept result text: "[openclaw] missing tool result in session history; ..."

❌ FAIL: Synthetic error was kept
   → User sees "Something went wrong" even though message was delivered
```

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```bash
git checkout fix/transcript-repair-prefer-real-result-84134
npx tsx proof-84419-runner.mts
```

- **Evidence after fix**:

```
$ npx tsx proof-84419-runner.mts  # on fix branch

--- Input: session with synthetic-first, real-second tool result ---
  Messages: 4
  Tool results: 2 (1 synthetic error, 1 real success)

--- Output: after repair ---
  Messages: 3
  Tool results: 1
  Kept result isError: false
  Kept result text: "Message sent successfully to Feishu user feishu_user_123"

✅ PASS: Real result preferred over synthetic error

--- Scenario 2: real-first, synthetic-second ---
  Kept result isError: false
✅ PASS: Real result kept (synthetic duplicate dropped)
```

- **Observed result after fix**: The repair function correctly identifies synthetic "missing tool result" markers and replaces them with real results when both exist for the same tool call ID. Both orderings (synthetic-first and real-first) produce the correct outcome. The user sees the successful delivery message instead of "Something went wrong."

- **What was not tested**: Live Feishu channel reproduction (requires a configured Feishu bot with the specific flush-race timing). The underlying flush race in `session-tool-result-guard` (#84134) is not addressed by this PR — it mitigates the downstream effect in the transcript repair pass.

## Merge note

⚠️ The first commit message contains `Fixes #84134`. When squash-merging, please change the merge message to use `Refs #84134` instead, so the root-cause Feishu issue remains open.
target.author: jerry-xin

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
